content:
# Qurayyah Sabkha

**Summary**: A salt flat and low depression on the western coast of the Arabian Gulf.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1994-al-guwaizani|Al-Guwaizani, 1994]] studied the qurayyah-sabkha to characterize its mineralogy and brine chemistry.

## Geological and Structural Setting

- [[1994-al-guwaizani|Al-Guwaizani, 1994]] Low-lying coastal plain

## Formations Present

- [[1994-al-guwaizani|Al-Guwaizani, 1994]] None specified as a sedimentary unit.

## Key Findings From This Location

- The sabkha is approximately 5 km in diameter and features a thick crust of halite near the southeastern edge. ([[1994-al-guwaizani|Al-Guwaizani, 1994, p. 14]])
